 {"id":"bd-fy2cm","title":"Implement --dry-run and explicit allocation","description":"Implement --dry-run and explicit allocation modes.

## --dry-run Mode

Show allocation plan without executing:
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "planned_assignments": [
    {
      "pane": 2,
      "would_assign": "bd-92e1",
      "reason": "highest_unblocks (9)"
    }
  ]
}
```

Useful for:
- Reviewing before executing
- Integration testing
- What-if analysis

## Explicit Allocation (--allocation)

Accept JSON mapping of pane to bead:
```bash
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --allocation='{"2":"bd-92e1","3":"bd-55pm"}'
```

Bypasses bv triage, uses exactly what user specifies.

## --skip-panes Flag

Exclude specific panes from allocation:
```bash
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv --skip-panes=4,10
```

Useful when some panes are:
- Already working on something
- Rate limited (leave them alone)
- Reserved for manual control","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:40.064506799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:19.677061450Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:19.677020303Z","close_reason":"Implemented --dry-run and --allocation flags for bulk assign","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fy2cm","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:40.094122872Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fzhuh","title":"Implement --fix auto-remediation","description":"Implement --fix flag for automatic remediation.

## Behavior

When --fix is provided:
1. Run normal diagnosis
2. For each auto_fixable recommendation, execute fix_command
3. Report results of each fix attempt

## What Gets Fixed

- crashed: Restart agent via --robot-restart-pane
- unresponsive: Kill and restart via --robot-restart-pane

## What Does NOT Get Fixed

- rate_limited: Requires human decision (wait vs caam)
- unknown: Not enough info to act safely

## Output Enhancement

Add fix_results to response:
```json
{
  "recommendations": [...],
  "fix_results": [
    {
      "pane": 15,
      "attempted": true,
      "success": true,
      "command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 15",
      "output": {...}
    }
  ]
}
```

## Safety

- Only fix auto_fixable=true items
- Run fixes sequentially (avoid overwhelming system)
- Report partial success if some fixes fail","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:46.095589296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.652649613Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.652594890Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fzhuh","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:34:25.238926656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fzhuh","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:46.124179988Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gf86b","title":"Task: Unit tests for Progressive CLI Discovery System","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for all components of the tiered CLI discovery system.

## Test Coverage Required

### 1. Tier Taxonomy Tests (taxonomy_test.go)
- TestTierEnum: Verify tier values (Apprentice=1, Journeyman=2, Master=3)
- TestCommandTierRegistry: All commands have tier assignments
- TestTierCompleteness: No commands missing from registry
- TestTierProgression: Each tier is superset of previous
- TestGetCommandsForTier: Returns correct commands per tier

### 2. Proficiency Config Tests (proficiency_test.go)
- TestLoadConfig: Load from valid config file
- TestLoadConfig_Missing: Handle missing file (create defaults)
- TestLoadConfig_Corrupted: Handle malformed JSON gracefully
- TestSaveConfig: Persist config correctly
- TestGetTier_EnvOverride: NTM_PROFICIENCY_TIER takes precedence
- TestGetTier_Default: New users get Tier 1
- TestUsageStats_Increment: Stats update correctly

### 3. Tiered Help Tests (help_test.go)
- TestRenderHelp_Tier1: Only essential commands shown
- TestRenderHelp_Tier2: Standard commands included
- TestRenderHelp_Tier3: All commands including robot mode
- TestRenderHelp_AllFlag: --all shows everything
- TestContextualTips: Tips match current state
- TestHelpFormatting: Output is readable, properly aligned

### 4. Level Command Tests (level_test.go)
- TestLevelShow: Displays current tier and stats
- TestLevelUp: Promotes to next tier
- TestLevelUp_AtMax: Handles already at Master
- TestLevelDown: Demotes to previous tier
- TestLevelDown_AtMin: Handles already at Apprentice
- TestLevelSet: Set specific tier by name or number

### 5. Usage Tracking Tests (tracking_test.go)
- TestTrackCommand: Commands increment counters
- TestPromotionCriteria_Tier1To2: Correct thresholds
- TestPromotionCriteria_Tier2To3: Correct thresholds
- TestPromotionSuggestion: Suggestion appears when criteria met
- TestPromotionSuggestion_RateLimit: No spam

### 6. Contextual Suggestions Tests (suggestions_test.go)
- TestSuggest_NoSessions: Suggests spawn
- TestSuggest_IdleAgents: Suggests send
- TestSuggest_BusyAgents: Suggests status/dashboard
- TestSuggest_RecentErrors: Suggests tail/logs
- TestSuggest_Disabled: Respects config disable

## Test Infrastructure
- Use testify/assert for assertions
- Table-driven tests where applicable
- Mock filesystem for config tests
- Test logging output for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- 90%+ code coverage for all new modules
- All tests pass in CI
- Tests run in <5 seconds total
- No flaky tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:19:55.405581503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:24:19.919522479Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:24:19.919356066Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive unit tests for Progressive CLI Discovery System:

 {"id":"bd-h1fb","title":"Task: Query CM for procedural memories","description":"## Purpose
Retrieve relevant procedural memories from CASS Memory (CM) for the current project.

## Scope
- Execute: cm context '<project>: starting new coding session' --json
- Parse response for relevantBullets, antiPatterns, historySnippets
- Cap at reasonable limit (10 rules) to avoid context bloat
- Handle CM unavailable gracefully

## CM Response Structure
{
    relevantBullets: [{id, content, category}],
    antiPatterns: [{id, content}],
    historySnippets: [{session, description}],
    suggestedCassQueries: [string]
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Memories included in recovery context
- Rules formatted with IDs for agent reference
- Anti-patterns highlighted distinctly
- Graceful degradation when CM unavailable

## Technical Notes
Create internal/cm/client.go for CM interaction
Use exec.Command for cm binary invocation
Set timeout to avoid blocking","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:40.762903869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:59:33.555125880Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:59:33.555048043Z","close_reason":"Implemented CLIClient in internal/cm/client.go with GetContext, GetRecoveryContext, and FormatForRecovery methods.  {"id":"bd-h3ha","title":"Add ntm send --randomize - randomize order of individualized prompts to avoid thundering herd on same files","description":"## Overview
Add --randomize flag to ntm send to randomize the order of individualized prompts, avoiding thundering herd problems when multiple agents start working on overlapping files.

## Problem
When sending individualized prompts (e.g., each agent gets a different bead), agents often start simultaneously on related files. This causes:
- Git conflicts when multiple agents edit the same area
- File reservation conflicts in Agent Mail
- Wasted work from merge conflicts

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --randomize` shuffles the order of prompts before sending.

## Implementation
```go
func randomizePrompts(prompts []Prompt) []Prompt {
    shuffled := make([]Prompt, len(prompts))
    copy(shuffled, prompts)
    rand.Shuffle(len(shuffled), func(i, j int) {
        shuffled[i], shuffled[j] = shuffled[j], shuffled[i]
    })
    return shuffled
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Randomize prompt order
ntm send myproject --randomize

# Combine with batch
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --randomize

# Combine with delay for extra staggering  
ntm send myproject --randomize --delay 2s
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompts shuffled before sending
- [ ] Deterministic seed option for reproducibility (--seed=N)
- [ ] Works with --batch and --distribute
- [ ] Logging shows shuffle order
- [ ] No effect if only one prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Shuffle produces different order
- Unit test: Seed produces reproducible order
- E2E test: Randomized batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
